pr.title: Match and improve hsd_3AA7, quatlib, and ftCo functions
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: Merged matching-focused rewrites across baselib CARD/save code, quaternion conversion, and one ftCommon shouldered function. The PR produced 7 new 100% matches and 13 measured improvements, with the largest work in `src/sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3AA7.c`; the decomp.dev GALE01 report showed matched code +0.06% / +2332 bytes.

key_files.role: Main batch: 1653 added / 865 deleted across 94 hunks, matching several CARD command/save helper functions and improving the large dispatcher and builders.
Improved `MatToQuat` from 95.55% to 99.92% by changing local layout, scalar temporaries, and expression shape; TODO notes stack frame still 8 bytes too large.
Matched `ftCo_8009C744` by stack padding and restoring/reshaping collision and victim calls.

what_changed: `ftCo_8009C744` reached 100.00%: replaced a dummy initialized stack byte array with `PAD_STACK(8)`, called `ftCo_800DC920(fp->victim_gobj, gobj)`, and restored `ftColl_80078710(vic_gobj, gobj, &fp->dmg.facing_dir_1)`.

`hsd_803AAA48` was substantially reshaped from 28.60% to 77.68%: `hsd_804D7980` became `volatile s32`, command type is read as `cmd->type`, dispatch uses `switch ((u32) type)`, command cases were reordered/rebuilt, and completions now consistently clear `cmd->type = 0`.

The CARD dispatcher and helpers moved many accesses from cached locals such as `state` or `entry->x10` to direct `cmd->state` / `cmd->type` accesses, apparently to match aliasing and live-range behavior.

Several command builders stopped using compound literals or zero-initialized `s32 cmd[9] = { 0 }` arrays and instead used local command arrays with explicit field writes, affecting stack and initialization codegen.
Loop bound expression order was normalized in multiple places from `(state->x24 + state->x8 + 0x2F) / state->x8` to `(0x2F + state->x24 + state->x8) / state->x8`.
`fn_803AC168` now snapshots `hsd_804D7984` into `idx` before incrementing and copies the command buffer to `hsd_804D1148[idx]`, improving queue write codegen.
`fn_803ACB74` was changed to `inline`, and duplicated `blocks_before` logic in `fn_803AD16C` was replaced with `fn_803AC6B8(state, file_id)`.

`MatToQuat` improved to 99.92% by moving `q3` earlier in local declarations, replacing `sc0/sc1/sc2` with direct diagonal division expressions and one `scale` temporary, and adjusting parentheses around the square-root expression.
smart_moves: The PR treated matching as source-shape work: it changed expression ordering, variable declaration order, local live ranges, and direct struct access patterns rather than inventing new abstractions.
Using `volatile` for `hsd_804D7980` is a precise type qualifier fix for a global command-queue index read/written around interrupt-sensitive code.
Replacing fake stack storage in `ftCo_8009C744` with `PAD_STACK(8)` kept the intended stack effect while removing misleading dummy data.
The dispatcher rewrite preserved CARD retry/error patterns while moving command-specific read/write/header-building paths into case shapes that match the original control flow better.
Queue rollback paths in `fn_803ADE4C`, `fn_803B1F78`, and `fn_803B21E8` used separate `snapN`/`savedN` locals and inline casts to `hsd_804D1138`, a useful live-range tactic for MWCC matching.
The author verified with `python configure.py --require-protos`, `ninja`, and decomp.dev GALE01 reports, giving measurable evidence for both new matches and partial improvements.
decomp_lessons: For MWCC matching, mathematically equivalent arithmetic is not interchangeable; even additive order in loop bounds can affect generated code.
Avoid over-caching struct pointers when matching old code. Direct `cmd->state` accesses can produce different alias/lifetime codegen than a `CardState* state` temporary.
Stack padding should use project macros such as `PAD_STACK` once the needed pad size is understood, instead of dummy initialized arrays.

Command buffer construction is sensitive to initialization style. Compound literals and `{ 0 }` array initialization may generate extra stores or different stack lifetimes compared with explicit slot writes.
Inlining tiny comparison helpers can be a matching lever when a call boundary prevents the expected branch/control-flow shape.
Header/struct evidence is limited in this slice: no header files changed; the visible type lesson is the in-file extern qualifier change from `s32 hsd_804D7980` to `volatile s32 hsd_804D7980`.
`MatToQuat` shows that floating-point local ordering and temporary reuse can nearly finish a match while leaving only stack-frame size differences.
naming_conventions: Unknown-address function names were preserved rather than speculatively renamed: `hsd_803AAA48`, `fn_803ACF30`, `fn_803ADE4C`, `fn_803B21E8`, `fn_803B1F78`, `hsd_803B2374`, `fn_803AC168`.

CARD/save structs continue to use known type names where available (`CardState`, `CardBufEntry`, `CardCmd`) while unresolved fields remain offset-style names such as `x0`, `x8`, `x10`, `x20`, `x24`, `x170`, and `x270`.
Fighter code keeps existing local naming style: `gobj`, `fp`, `vic_gobj`, `vic_fp`, and `victim_gobj`.
Local names such as `blocks_before`, `file_blocks`, `hdr_offset`, `rem`, `scale`, `q3`, `snap1`, and `saved1` encode either domain meaning or intentional live-range separation.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: `PAD_STACK(8)`, `PAD_STACK(4)`, `PAD_STACK(12)`, and `PAD_STACK(16)` were used to control stack layout where necessary.
Changed `switch (type)` to `switch ((u32) type)` and added a `default: return result;` path in `hsd_803AAA48`.
Expanded boolean expressions such as `hsd_804D799C == 1` into explicit `if/else` assignment to force the desired code shape.
Used direct array indexed writes like `cmd->state->x0[hdr_offset + 0x10]` instead of a temporary pointer such as `u8* d`.
Snapshot queue indices before mutation, e.g. `s32 idx = hsd_804D7984; hsd_804D7984 = ...; memcpy(... hsd_804D1148[idx] ...)`.
Replaced cached `entries` usage in rollback loops with inline `((CardBufEntry*) hsd_804D1138)[saved].x10 = 0` casts where that matched better.
Reordered locals in `MatToQuat` and collapsed separate scale temporaries into one `scale` variable.
Removed zero initialization from several `s32 cmd[9]` locals and explicitly assigned only the fields needed by the command path.
review_feedback: Human review feedback in the slice is minimal: jurrejelle approved the PR with an empty review body and there were no inline review comments.
Automated decomp.dev feedback reported 7 new matches and 13 unmatched-item improvements; `MatToQuat` remained just short of full match at 99.92%.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish `MatToQuat` from 99.92% to 100% or resolve the remaining 8-byte stack-frame mismatch.
Search future `hsd_3AA7.c` work for `CardCmd` and `CardState` field naming improvements after these command-dispatch matches.
Compare other PRs that use `cmd[9]` command buffer construction to see when explicit field writes beat `{ 0 }` initialization.
Look for follow-up changes to `hsd_803AAA48` to confirm whether the dispatcher case order and `cmd->state` access pattern stayed stable.
Search `ftCo_8009C744` and `ftColl_80078710` in later fighter PRs to verify no semantic regression from restoring the collision call.
